title: Stage 3 forward plan: dual-timescale I with cycle-boundary adaptation (revised after adversarial review)

content.purpose: Forward plan for ANIMA Stage 3, revised after adversarial review. Synthesized from constraint taxonomy (48 constraints), 7+2 Framework mapping, Stage 2 vacuousness root cause, and analytical derivation of signal properties.
content.central_problem: Stage 3 requires self-generated adaptation signal for w_lr. The timescale barrier (c048) means no per-step signal works. Analytical derivation shows across-K err variance is monotonically decreasing in w_lr (not peaked at interior optimum). The interior optimum may exist only in MI space, not in prediction-error space.

architecture_decision.chosen: Dual-timescale I (augment existing I with slow component)
architecture_decision.rejected: T-as-Phi (new state variable with own dynamics)
architecture_decision.rationale: Stage 3 makes adaptation rate adaptive — does not add new variables. T-as-Phi would add a new frozen frame element without eliminating two, violating Principle IV. T-as-Phi belongs to Stage 4+. Dual-timescale I maps to 7+2 calcium [Ca2+]i: two timescales of the same variable (fast entry, slow clearance).

primary_approach.name: Dual-timescale I with cycle-boundary adaptation (Approach A revised)
primary_approach.mechanism: Add mem_slow alongside existing mem in anima_organism.py. I_fast (tau=0.3) tracks per-step err. I_slow (tau_slow=0.001-0.01) accumulates across ~100-1000 steps. At cycle boundary (end of K-signal permutation), I_slow state drives w_lr adaptation.
primary_approach.seven_plus_two_mapping: I_fast = fast calcium entry (err detection). I_slow = slow calcium clearance (amplitude x duration integral). Cycle boundary trigger = callback, not a new T variable. T-as-Phi deferred to Stage 4+.
primary_approach.principle_II_compliance: I_slow driven by same err variable as I_fast and W. No external evaluator. Adaptation signal is byproduct of computation.
primary_approach.avoids_constraints: Not per-step (avoids c048). Fires at cycle boundaries only. No new frozen frame element added.

analytical_findings.err_variance_monotonic: Across-K err variance is monotonically DECREASING in w_lr. High w_lr = steep err decay within signal windows = high across-K variance. Low w_lr = flat err = low variance. Does NOT peak at interior optimum 0.0003.
analytical_findings.ratio_hypothesis_weakened: I_slow/I_fast_mean ratio does NOT cleanly peak at interior optimum. Both low w_lr (large I_slow from persistence, moderate I_fast) and high w_lr (moderate I_slow, small I_fast) produce high ratios. Ratio is U-shaped or monotonic, not peaked.
analytical_findings.structural_decoupling: The interior optimum may exist in MI space (sequence distinguishability) but NOT in err space (prediction error). MI gap measures a global property; err measures a local property. The relationship is non-monotonic in MI but potentially monotonic in err.
analytical_findings.equilibrium_hypothesis: A monotonic signal may still have a natural fixed point near the optimum if there are countervailing boundary forces: at very high w_lr, W oscillates/diverges (err explodes, downward pressure); at very low w_lr, err is pure noise (I accumulates garbage, upward pressure). The equilibrium between these pressures may naturally sit near the optimum. KEY OPEN QUESTION for Phase 1.

B_I_curvature.name: I trajectory curvature (single-variable, no T)
B_I_curvature.risk: 1D cycle-level signal. Likely monotonic — same limitation as ratio.

C_W_velocity.name: W convergence rate
C_W_velocity.risk: c026 lesson: parameter activity does not imply optimal performance.

phase_gate.phase_1_analytical_then_empirical: Session 21. (a) Derive I_slow(w_lr) and I_fast_mean(w_lr) shapes analytically — confirm monotonicity. (b) Derive whether adaptation dynamics d(w_lr) = f(I_slow, I_fast) has a natural equilibrium (boundary forces creating fixed point). (c) 2-seed empirical characterization at w_lr = [0.0001, 0.0003, 0.001, 0.003, 0.01]: measure I_slow, I_fast_mean, I_curvature, W_velocity. (d) Normalize by cycle length for K-confound.
phase_gate.phase_2_binding: 5-seed paired diagnostic. Does adaptive w_lr converge to values near 0.0003? Non-trivial, non-degenerate behavior?
phase_gate.phase_3_validation: 10-seed multi-K paired design. Dual-I-adaptive vs fixed w_lr=0.0003.

open_questions: Does the adaptation dynamics d(w_lr) = f(I_slow, I_fast) have a natural equilibrium near 0.0003 due to boundary forces (W divergence at high w_lr, noise-dominated err at low w_lr)?
Is the w_lr interior optimum structurally invisible from within err-space? If so, is w_lr a calibration parameter at ALL stages — not an adaptation target?
If w_lr is not adaptable, which frozen frame element should Stage 3 target instead? (gamma, tau, structural constant?)
content.vacuousness_criterion: If Phase 1 shows (a) all internal signals are monotonic in w_lr with no peaked structure, AND (b) no natural equilibrium exists in the adaptation dynamics, Stage 3 for w_lr is structurally vacuous. The inverted-U landscape has no intrinsic gradient detectable from within. This would be a significant theoretical result: the MI-err structural decoupling.
